title: Your Financial Aid Offer
text:
Your Financial Aid Offer
View Your Financial Aid Offer
The 2026-2027 estimated cost of attendance and financial aid offer (if you applied for financial aid by all stated deadlines) is available for students in the NYU Albert Student Center. Click on the "Finances" tab under the appropriate academic year.
For more information about available financial aid programs, please visit NYU’s Types of Aid page and click on the Shanghai section.
U.S. Federal Loans (U.S. Citizens and Permanent Residents)
If you have been offered U.S. federal loans, you (the student) must accept/decline all or a portion of your loans in NYU Albert under the Accept/Decline Awards link.
If you plan to use U.S. federal aid, you must:
Meet Satisfactory Academic Progress standards
Meet Satisfactory Academic Progress standards
File the FAFSA annually
File the FAFSA annually
For detailed information about federal loans, please visit NYU’s Federal Direct Loans page.
Renewal of NYU Shanghai Scholarships/Grants
NYU Shanghai scholarship and grant aid is renewed annually at the same amount, provided you maintain full-time enrollment (12 credits or more per semester).
More detailed information about renewability and academic progress standards is available on the Returning Students webpage.
Outside Scholarships or External Funding
All students who expect to receive financial support from outside their family, such as government grants, community scholarships, or other sources, must notify the Office of Financial Aid at shanghai.financial.support@nyu.edu. External funding will be considered when determining your final aid eligibility.
Questions About Your Financial Aid Offer?
Contact the NYU Shanghai Office of Financial Aid:
Phone: +1 212-998-2838Email: shanghai.financial.support@nyu.eduOffice Hours: Monday–Friday, 10:00 am–4:00 pm Eastern Time
